# Satoshi API

Satoshi API turns a Bitcoin Core full node into a production-ready REST API.
It handles caching, rate limiting, authentication, and WebSocket streaming
so developers and AI agents can query the Bitcoin network without running
their own node.

## Base URL

https://bitcoinsapi.com

## Features

- 74 REST endpoints covering blocks, transactions, mempool, fees, mining,
  UTXO analysis, network stats, supply tracking, and address lookups
- Real-time WebSocket streaming (new blocks, fee changes, mempool activity)
- Fee estimation with historical percentile data
- Mempool monitoring (size, fee distribution, pending transactions)
- Block exploration (by height or hash, with transaction details)
- Transaction lookup and raw broadcast
- Mining stats (difficulty, hashrate, pool identification)
- UTXO set analysis and SegWit adoption metrics
- Prometheus metrics endpoint

## Getting Started

1. Register for a free API key (no credit card required):
   POST https://bitcoinsapi.com/api/v1/register

2. Include your key in requests:
   X-API-Key: your_key_here

3. Interactive API docs:
   https://bitcoinsapi.com/docs

## SDKs and Tools

- Python SDK: pip install satoshi-api
- MCP server for AI agents: pip install bitcoin-mcp
- Self-hosted option: clone the repo and point at your own Bitcoin Core node

## Rate Limits

- Anonymous: lightweight GET endpoints only, 144-block cap
- Free tier: all endpoints, 144-block cap per request
- Pro tier: higher limits, 1008-block cap
- Enterprise: custom limits, 2016-block cap

## Contact

api@bitcoinsapi.com
